reasoning: The Lean statement captures all three parts of the informal claim: irreducibility of p = X^3 - 2, irreducibility of q = X^3 + 2, and isomorphism of the quotient rings. Parameterizing p and q with equality hypotheses is equivalent to fixing them to the specific polynomials. `span {p}` (with `open Ideal`) is the ideal generated by p, matching (p(x)). `Nonempty (_ ≃+* _)` expresses existence of a ring isomorphism, which is the standard meaning of "isomorphic"; since p and q are irreducible the quotients are fields, so a ring isomorphism is automatically a field isomorphism. No hypotheses are dropped or added.
